title: NOC 72501 – Water well drillers | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Water Well Drillers (NOC 72501) are the essential technical specialists securing Canada’s most vital resource: clean groundwater. As rural expansion continues and climate-driven water security becomes a national priority, these professionals are in high demand to operate specialized mobile drilling rigs. Operating at the TEER 2 level, modern drillers utilize GPS-integrated terrain mapping, digital flow-rate sensors, and advanced environmental protection protocols. In 2026, the role has expanded to include the installation of geothermal earth loops, positioning drillers at the forefront of Canada’s green energy transition.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Category2 (Occupations usually requiring college, apprenticeship, or supervisory experience)Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officers and controllersSub-Major Group725 – Surveyors, extractors and other technical occupations

duties: Rig Operation: Operating a variety of mobile water well drilling rigs (rotary, cable tool, or hammer) to drill, monitor, and maintain wells.
Site Preparation: Setting up drilling rigs and equipment, and selecting appropriate drill bits and casings based on geological data.
Environmental Testing: Collecting and logging core samples and performing pumping tests to determine water yield and quality.
System Installation: Installing well screens, casings, and specialized pumps to complete the well assembly.
Maintenance & Repair: Performing mechanical maintenance on rigs and troubleshooting drilling obstructions or equipment failures.
Geothermal Work: Drilling and installing loops for ground-source heat pump systems.
employment_requirements: Education: Completion of secondary school is typically required.
Apprenticeship: Completion of a two- to three-year Water Well Drilling Apprenticeship program OR two to three years of work experience combined with industry courses is mandatory.
Certification: Trade certification is voluntary in BC, AB, SK, ON, and NB, but it is highly recommended for higher-paying government contracts.
Regulatory Licensing: In most provinces (e.g., Ontario’s Ministry of the Environment), a specific Well Technician License is mandatory to legally lead a drilling operation.
Red Seal: While not a Red Seal trade nationally, provincial certifications are often recognized across borders through the Internal Trade Agreement.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook is Moderate to Strong, particularly in provinces with significant agricultural and remote residential sectors:

Ontario: Very Good prospects. In February 2026, Ontario specifically included NOC 72501 in targeted OINP invitations under the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker and International Student streams. On March 25, 2026, the province issued further invitations for high-demand occupations in Central and Eastern Ontario, where groundwater reliance is highest.

British Columbia: High Priority. Water well drillers are currently listed as a Priority Occupation in BC. With a median wage of $43.00/hr, the province is actively recruiting for those capable of navigating BC’s complex mountainous hydrogeology.

Alberta: Strongest Demand. Alberta remains the national leader in drilling activity. The province reports a consistent need for drillers to support both the agricultural sector and the growing Earth Loop Technician specialization for geothermal heating.

National Trend: While the work is seasonal and physically demanding, over 90% of drillers are employed full-time during the peak season. The 2026 market highly favors those with dual certification in Well Pump Installation.
Why Work in Canada as a Water Well Driller?
Direct Environmental Impact: Lead the effort to provide sustainable water solutions for residential, commercial, and industrial clients across Canada.
Technological Specialization: Gain experience with 2026-grade technologies, including Sonic Drilling for high-accuracy sampling and automated mud-cycling systems.
Excellent Compensation: As a TEER 2 trade, your earning potential is high, with a national median wage of $31.00/hr and top-tier earners in BC and Alberta reaching over $90,000 annually.
Priority for PR: In 2026, this NOC is a core feature of OINP targeted draws and is eligible for the Express Entry "Trade Occupations" category.
pathways: OINP Employer Job Offer: In February and March 2026, OINP issued invitations to candidates in NOC 72501 with a score of 53+ (Southwestern Ontario) and 55+ (Central Ontario). This is a prime route for those with an Ontario job offer.
Express Entry (Trade Occupations Category): Updated for 2026. Eligible candidates now require 12 months of full-time work experience (up from 6 months in 2025) within the last 3 years.
2026 TR to PR Pathway: A confirmed initiative targeting up to 33,000 temporary residents. Water well drillers are classified as "High-Priority Infrastructure and Resource Workers."
BC PNP Skilled Worker: A reliable route for drillers with a job offer from a BC-based environmental or drilling firm, particularly in the "Interior" and "Vancouver Island" regions.
